Details: One study reported effective removal of chlortetracycline by granular activated carbon (GAC) [1526]. Two GACs were tested, a coal-based carbon (Calgon F-400, U.S.A.) and a coconut-based carbon (Samchully, Korea). Chlortetracycline removal was 85 percent for the coal-based carbon and 80 percent for the coconut-based carbon with a contact time of 10 minutes.
